evals.prompt: Scaffold the structure for the order-export feature. The design is in .docs/order-export-design.md.
evals.expected_output: Reads the design doc, brainstorms only to fill structural gaps, gets explicit lock confirmation, proposes the file/interface layout for approval, then writes stub-only files (no logic, no tests) into the source tree and stops without committing.
expectations: Reads .docs/order-export-design.md as the source of truth before proposing any structure
Holds a lock gate — does not generate scaffold until the user explicitly confirms decisions are locked
Generated method bodies are non-implementing stubs (NotImplementedException / NotImplementedError / throw Error 'Not implemented'), with zero business logic
Writes files into the real source layout but does not stage or create a git commit
Does not write unit tests or implementation code

evals.prompt: Create the scaffold for work item 1461.
evals.expected_output: Fetches the work item content via the azdevops-operations skill, interviews the user on anything ambiguous rather than guessing, locks decisions, then scaffolds structure-only files.
expectations: Resolves the numeric id by fetching the work item (via azdevops-operations) instead of guessing its content
Interviews the user to close gaps when the requirement or approach is unclear — does not infer silently
Proceeds to scaffold only after decisions are locked, producing stub-only members

evals.prompt: Stub out the new pricing service. I haven't written anything down yet.
evals.expected_output: Because no design doc or work item exists, runs the built-in requirement/approach interview, presents approach options with trade-offs, captures a Locked Decisions summary (optionally to .docs/), then proposes and writes the scaffold.
expectations: Detects there is no design input and runs the brainstorm interview rather than assuming a structure
Presents one or more approaches with trade-offs and obtains an explicit approach choice
Captures locked decisions before scaffolding and detects the repo stack to choose the correct stub idiom

non_goals: Writing unit tests (that is the developer's Step 4)
Writing implementation logic or filling stub bodies (Step 5)
Creating a git commit — the skill writes files and leaves staging/commit to the user
Guessing requirements or approach when the design input is missing or ambiguous
Running downstream workflow steps (code review, PR creation, QA verification)
